item.title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

item.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

item.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Digg returns (again) as another place to read AI news.

item.title: ‘Too early’ to talk IPO, Redwood Materials’ incoming CFO says
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The battery-recycling and energy-storage company has hired former Tesla finance chief Deepak Ahuja, reuniting him with former Tesla CTO JB Straubel.

item.title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

item.title: TikTok launches an ad-free subscription plan in the UK
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Users who sign up for the plan won’t see ads on TikTok, and their data won’t be used for advertising purposes.

item.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

item.title: Discord launches Nitro Rewards, giving subscribers access to the base tier of Xbox Game Pass for no extra cost
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Discord's Nitro Rewards program will give Nitro subscribers access to Xbox Game Pass and discounts from Logitech, SteelSeries, and other gaming brands.

item.title: Venmo’s biggest makeover in years comes at a very interesting time
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The timing is notable. PayPal, which owns Venmo, is restructuring to spin Venmo off as a stand-alone business unit — a move widely seen as laying the groundwork for a potential sale. Stripe has reportedly expressed interest in buying PayPal outright.

item.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

item.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

item.title: Uber has always wanted to be more than a ride; now it has reason to hurry
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The company has been trying to embed itself inside the AV industry — as a data provider, an investor, and a distribution platform — but the consumer-facing bet may be just as important.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Lime’s IPO gamble
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

item.title: The hottest place for startups to strike a deal? The F1 paddock
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: F1 Grands Prix have emerged as a new place to see and be seen if you are a founder or investor.

item.title: Voice AI in India is hard — Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

item.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
item.dc:creator: Natasha Lomas, Romain Dillet, Kyle Wiggers, Lucas Ropek
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Parker, a well-funded startup offering corporate credit cards and banking services, has filed for bankruptcy and is widely reported to have shut down.

item.title: GM agrees to pay $12.75M in California driver privacy settlement
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: General Motors has reached a privacy-related settlement with a group of law enforcement agencies led by California attorney general Rob Bonta.
